overview.short: Bhumara Temple, sometimes called Bhumra, Bhubhara or Bharkuleswar, is a 5th or 6th-century Gupta era Hindu stone temple site dedicated to Shiva near Satna, in the Indian state Madh
overview.description: Bhumara Temple, sometimes called Bhumra, Bhubhara or Bharkuleswar, is a 5th or 6th-century Gupta era Hindu stone temple site dedicated to Shiva near Satna, in the Indian state Madhya Pradesh. The temple has a square plan with a sanctum and Mandapa. While much of it is in ruins, enough of the temple structure and works of art have survived for scholarly studies. The temple is notable as one of the early examples of an architecture that included an enclosed concentric pradakshina-patha (circumambulation path).

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Shiva temple, Bhumara?
faq.a: The best time to visit Shiva temple, Bhumara is Oct – Mar. Summers are 25–42°C and winters are 7–24°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Shiva temple, Bhumara?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Jabalpur Airport (151 km) and the nearest railway station is Satna Junction (25 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Shiva temple, Bhumara?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Shiva temple, Bhumara at a relaxed pace.

faq.a: Stays in Shiva temple, Bhumara start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.
